---
title: "Polls US Election 2016 Analysis"
author: "Emre Türkoğlu"
format: html
editor: visual
---

install.packages("knitr")
install.packages("rmarkdown")

{r}
library(dslabs)

data("polls_us_election_2016")

head(polls_us_election_2016)

my_first <- "Emre"
my_birth_year <- 1987

data("polls_us_election_2016")
data <- polls_us_election_2016

k <- (nchar(my_first) + my_birth_year) %% 15 + 8
k

if (k %% 2 == 0) {
  head(data, k)
} else {
  tail(data, k)
}

sum(is.na(data))

na_per_column <- colSums(is.na(data))
na_per_column_sorted <- sort(na_per_column, decreasing = TRUE)

head(na_per_column_sorted, 8)

new_data <- data

for(col in names(new_data)){

  if(is.numeric(new_data[[col]])){

    new_data[[col]][is.na(new_data[[col]])] <- my_birth_year + k

  } else if(is.character(new_data[[col]]) | is.factor(new_data[[col]])){

    new_data[[col]][is.na(new_data[[col]])] <- paste0(my_first, "_", k)

  }

}

if (k %% 2 == 0) {
  head(new_data, k)
} else {
  tail(new_data, k)
}

sum(is.na(new_data))

anyNA(new_data)
---

## Quarto

Quarto enables you to weave together content and executable code into a finished document. To learn more about Quarto see <https://quarto.org>.

## Running Code

When you click the **Render** button a document will be generated that includes both content and the output of embedded code. You can embed code like this:

```{r}
1 + 1
```

You can add options to executable code like this

```{r}
#| echo: false
2 * 2
```

The `echo: false` option disables the printing of code (only output is displayed).
